negative_cases.description: Last byte of the payload flipped; original signature reused.
negative_cases.name: descriptor_tampered_payload

negative_cases.description: Verified against a real but different Ed25519 public key than the one that signed it.
negative_cases.name: descriptor_wrong_key

negative_cases.description: The descriptor envelope's own (payload, signature) pair verified against a DIFFERENT context string ('linkkeys-local-rp-login-request') than the one it was actually signed under ('linkkeys-local-rp-descriptor'). Must fail: a signature over one structure must never verify as another.
negative_cases.name: descriptor_signature_under_context_linkkeys-local-rp-login-request

note: Every signature-input is CBOR([context: tstr, payload: bstr]) — a two-element CBOR array, context first — per Wire Precision "Signature input bytes". `payload_cbor_hex` is the exact bytes shipped in the envelope's payload-carrying field (see `envelope_field`); `signature_input_cbor_hex` is CBOR([context, payload_cbor_hex]); `signature_hex` is the Ed25519 signature over `signature_input_cbor_hex`. All four structures use Ed25519 only.
